title: ophrys bertolonii subsp. insengae
intro: Ophrys bertolonii subsp. insengae, also known as the Italian bee orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically as a treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Ophrys bertolonii subsp. insengae include infusions and decoctions, which are often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
